channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: House Democrats accuse Trump of 'hijacking' America's 250th birthday for his own gain
item.description: A 55-page report from House Democrats accuses Freedom 250 of America's birthday celebrations for profit, using questionable fundraising methods. Watchdog groups had already sounded alarms.
item.content:encoded:
A 55-page report from House Democrats accuses Freedom 250 of America's birthday celebrations for profit, using questionable fundraising methods. Watchdog groups had already sounded alarms.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: America 250: What's the vibe?
item.description: As America prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, are people excited and proud to celebrate? And how does it compare to the last major anniversary, the bicentennial in 1976?
item.content:encoded: As America prepares to celebrate its 250th anniversary, are people excited and proud to celebrate? And how does it compare to the last major anniversary, the bicentennial in 1976?
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: The effort to get benefits to American civilians exposed to toxic burn pits
item.description: A burn pit law from Congress left out thousands of American civilians working for the government who were exposed to the same toxins. Now there's an effort to change that.
item.content:encoded: A burn pit law from Congress left out thousands of American civilians working for the government who were exposed to the same toxins. Now there's an effort to change that.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Photos: Fans gather in the Bay Area for the World Cup match between the U.S. and Bosnia-Herzegovina
item.description: World Cup watch parties in Santa Clara and the San Francisco Bay Area brought fans together for the United States vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina match, where the U.S. earned a 2-0 win to reach the Round of 16.
item.content:encoded:
World Cup watch parties in Santa Clara and the San Francisco Bay Area brought fans together for the United States vs. Bosnia-Herzegovina match, where the U.S. earned a 2-0 win to reach the Round of 16.

(Image credit: Beth LaBerge)
item.dc:creator: Meredith Nierman

item.title: The days are hot, but so are the nights. That's a problem, experts say
item.description: As much of the Eastern U.S. experiences potentially record-breaking daytime temperatures, the nights are also staying unusually warm, leaving the human body no time to recover.
item.content:encoded:
As much of the Eastern U.S. experiences potentially record-breaking daytime temperatures, the nights are also staying unusually warm, leaving the human body no time to recover.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Former ethics lawyer says Trump's crypto poses 'clear conflict of interest'
item.description: Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."
item.content:encoded:
Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: New York City to hold multiple celebrations during scorching Fourth of July weekend
item.description: Fireworks, tall ships, a hotdog eating contest. New York City will host it all, as temperatures near 100 degrees this Fourth of July weekend.
item.content:encoded: Fireworks, tall ships, a hotdog eating contest. New York City will host it all, as temperatures near 100 degrees this Fourth of July weekend.
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: Federal civil rights data holds schools accountable. Under Trump, it's 6 months late
item.description: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
item.content:encoded: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump and his family took in over $1 billion in crypto earnings last year, Democratic Socialists pose challenge for Democratic party, U.S. defeats Bosnia-Herzegovina in World Cup knockout round.
item.content:encoded: Trump and his family took in over $1 billion in crypto earnings last year, Democratic Socialists pose challenge for Democratic party, U.S. defeats Bosnia-Herzegovina in World Cup knockout round.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Presidential library honoring Theodore Roosevelt opens in North Dakota
item.description: President Trump visited the nation's newest presidential library in North Dakota on Wednesday. It honors the legacy of Theodore Roosevelt. NPR received a tour of the new facility last week.
item.content:encoded: President Trump visited the nation's newest presidential library in North Dakota on Wednesday. It honors the legacy of Theodore Roosevelt. NPR received a tour of the new facility last week.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
